allowed: Public repository files and commit metadata
Abstracted decision records that contain no private chat, health, credential, or identifying personal content
forbidden: Credentials, tokens, account identifiers, or private contact data

records.title: Five autonomous teams share one durable state
records.summary: The repository defines five active teams and requires each to read shared state, update only owned records, and leave evidence plus a peer trigger request.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Re-read CURRENT_STATE.json at the indexed blob SHA and compare the operating_model, teams, and coordination_rule fields.

records.title: Public repositories must exclude private chat and health material
records.summary: Continuity artifacts may reference private-source categories abstractly but must not reproduce private chat or health content into the public repository.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Inspect hard_boundaries in CURRENT_STATE.json; reject this record if the boundary is absent or materially changed.

records.title: M-001 provenance-preserving continuity index
records.summary: The highest-priority continuity task requires an index schema, explicit source boundaries, and initial indexed records.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Re-read ACTIVE_QUEUE.json at the indexed blob SHA and verify item M-001 owner, priority, action, and required_evidence.

records.title: Specialists require distinct reusable responsibility
records.summary: A specialist can be added only when responsibility, interface, owner, acceptance test, and reusable output are distinct; duplicate or nonproductive roles are retired under the registry rule.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Compare this summary with the registry rule and retirement_rule at the indexed blob SHA.

records.title: No durable peer-trigger requests were pending at observation time
records.summary: The trigger request array was empty when inspected. This does not prove peers ran; it only records absence of durable pending requests at that blob.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Re-read the exact blob and verify requests is an empty array; later nonempty versions supersede only the temporal observation, not the historical record.

records.title: M-001 was completed as an additive three-commit continuity slice
records.summary: The first continuity slice added a schema, added an initial index, and then marked the owned queue item complete. The sequence preserves implementation history rather than collapsing it into one unsupported completion claim.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Fetch all three commits, verify their changed paths and order, and reject the record if any commit does not correspond to schema creation, index creation, or the M-001 queue update.

records.title: Evidence identity, provenance, trust policy, and claim truth are separate layers
records.summary: The project now treats trace correlation, exact-byte provenance, trusted-builder acceptance, and truth of the underlying runtime or research claim as distinct propositions. Passing an earlier layer must not be reported as proof of a later layer.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Inspect R-002 through R-004 and Cloudflare C-001 progress in the indexed queue blob; falsify this record if those artifacts merge correlation, provenance, policy acceptance, and claim truth into one status.

records.title: Cloudflare deployment remains unproven despite a complete readiness and proof pipeline
records.summary: Readiness classification, proof validation, attestation, and trusted-builder policy are implemented, but no resolving Cloudflare Pages URL, served identity, or deployed commit has been observed. The durable memory therefore preserves both implementation progress and the unresolved external deployment claim.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Resolve this record only with a retained deployment proof containing a resolving Cloudflare URL, matching served identity, and immutable source commit; implementation artifacts alone are insufficient.

records.title: M-001 completion supersedes its earlier active queue state
records.summary: CM-0003 correctly recorded M-001 as the highest-priority required continuity task at an earlier queue blob. The current queue records M-001 as completed with its schema, index, privacy boundary, commits, and verification. This later record supersedes only the current-status interpretation of CM-0003; it does not erase the earlier historical observation.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Fetch ACTIVE_QUEUE.json at blob abb807e1a25d399f301238f6b8cc26d6be10385f and verify M-001 status is completed with the recorded progress. Fetch CM-0003's older queue blob to confirm the prior required-state observation remains historically accurate.
